results.summary: Transformed: General refinement. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.95.
results.why: This pass is a small general refinement to improve efficiency and interaction without changing the deck's casual posture. [[Pongify]] gives Wan Shi Tong a cheap, flexible answer to problematic creatures, while trimming a lower-impact slot helps the list stay tighter after validation. The change supports smoother gameplay at a moderate budget.

results.summary: Transformed: Mana base improvement. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.97.
results.why: This pass improves the mana base by turning a basic land slot into [[Wayfarer's Bauble]], giving [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] a cheap, flexible way to find land development from the deck. The swap keeps the deck casual while slightly improving early consistency and smoothing future land drops.

raw.why: This pass improves the mana base by turning a basic land slot into [[Wayfarer's Bauble]], giving [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] a cheap, flexible way to find land development from the deck. The swap keeps the deck casual while slightly improving early consistency and smoothing future land drops.

added.wayfarer's bauble: [[Wayfarer's Bauble]] adds low-cost land ramp that helps smooth early mana development.

removed.island: One [[Island]] was cut to make room for ramp without significantly reducing blue access.

results.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.83.
results.why: This pass tightens the curve by replacing expensive, swingy interaction with cheaper answers that are easier to hold up. [[Reality Shift]] and [[Resculpt]] keep the deck responsive earlier in the game while supporting [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] at a more consistent casual pace. Cutting [[Commandeer]] and [[Tidespout Tyrant]] lowers clunky top-end without changing the mana base.

raw.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.83.
raw.plan: This pass tightens the curve by replacing expensive, swingy interaction with cheaper answers that are easier to hold up. [[Reality Shift]] and [[Resculpt]] keep the deck responsive earlier in the game while supporting [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] at a more consistent casual pace. Cutting [[Commandeer]] and [[Tidespout Tyrant]] lowers clunky top-end without changing the mana base.
raw.why: This pass tightens the curve by replacing expensive, swingy interaction with cheaper answers that are easier to hold up. [[Reality Shift]] and [[Resculpt]] keep the deck responsive earlier in the game while supporting [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] at a more consistent casual pace. Cutting [[Commandeer]] and [[Tidespout Tyrant]] lowers clunky top-end without changing the mana base.

added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] is efficient instant-speed removal that fits the lower curve.
added.resculpt: [[Resculpt]] adds another cheap answer to creatures or artifacts without heavy mana investment.

removed.commandeer: [[Commandeer]] is powerful but its high alternate cost makes it too clunky for this curve-tightening pass.
removed.tidespout tyrant: [[Tidespout Tyrant]] sits at the top end and was trimmed to reduce expensive spells.

warnings: Model output had 126 cards after color filter; list trimmed to 100.

Interaction guard removed additions that were not clear answers or protection.
results.summary: Transformed: More interaction. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.89.
results.why: This pass increases the deck’s ability to answer opposing threats at low cost, which better supports an interaction-focused [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] build. The additions emphasize efficient instant-speed disruption and removal, while the cuts trim slower or more self-contained cards that do less to protect your position. Overall, the swaps make the list more responsive without pushing beyond casual power.

raw.summary: Transformed: More interaction. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.89.
raw.plan: This pass increases the deck’s ability to answer opposing threats at low cost, which better supports an interaction-focused [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] build. The additions emphasize efficient instant-speed disruption and removal, while the cuts trim slower or more self-contained cards that do less to protect your position. Overall, the swaps make the list more responsive without pushing beyond casual power.
raw.why: This pass increases the deck’s ability to answer opposing threats at low cost, which better supports an interaction-focused [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] build. The additions emphasize efficient instant-speed disruption and removal, while the cuts trim slower or more self-contained cards that do less to protect your position. Overall, the swaps make the list more responsive without pushing beyond casual power.

added.cyclonic rift: [[Cyclonic Rift]] gives the deck a flexible instant-speed answer that can also reset opposing boards.
added.pongify: [[Pongify]] adds cheap creature removal that trades up on mana efficiently.
added.rapid hybridization: [[Rapid Hybridization]] provides another one-mana way to answer problematic creatures.
added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] offers clean exile-based creature interaction for threats blue normally struggles with.
added.spell pierce: [[Spell Pierce]] improves early interaction by cheaply checking key noncreature spells.
added.swan song: [[Swan Song]] adds efficient protection and stack interaction against important noncreature spells.

removed.triskaidekaphile: [[Triskaidekaphile]] is a slower value and alternate-win card that does not improve interaction density.
removed.twenty-toed toad: [[Twenty-Toed Toad]] is a self-contained payoff that contributes less to answering opponents.
removed.valley floodcaller: [[Valley Floodcaller]] is more synergy-focused than interactive for this pass.
removed.waterbending scroll: [[Waterbending Scroll]] is less direct as an answer than the added interaction pieces.
removed.windfall: [[Windfall]] is a refill effect that is less reliable as interaction in this build.
removed.wizard class: [[Wizard Class]] is a slower setup card that makes room for more immediate answers.

warnings: Color identity validation removed 1 card line(s) from the model output (off-color or unknown).
Model output had 126 cards after color filter; list trimmed to 100.
Interaction guard removed additions that were not clear answers or protection.

cautions: Budget route returned no one-for-one swaps.
results.summary: Budget swaps returned no safe cheaper pairs.

raw.summary: Budget swaps returned no safe cheaper pairs.

warnings: Model output had 107 cards after color filter; list trimmed to 100.
results.summary: Transformed: More casual. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~3.06.
results.why: These swaps make [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] play in a more casual, interactive way by replacing combo-leaning and high-polarity pieces with straightforward card draw, bounce, and creature answers. The added cards keep the deck flexible without pushing explosive turns or lock patterns. Overall, the pass lowers swinginess and makes the game plan easier for a casual table to engage with.

raw.summary: Transformed: More casual. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~3.06.
raw.plan: These swaps make [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] play in a more casual, interactive way by replacing combo-leaning and high-polarity pieces with straightforward card draw, bounce, and creature answers. The added cards keep the deck flexible without pushing explosive turns or lock patterns. Overall, the pass lowers swinginess and makes the game plan easier for a casual table to engage with.
raw.why: These swaps make [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] play in a more casual, interactive way by replacing combo-leaning and high-polarity pieces with straightforward card draw, bounce, and creature answers. The added cards keep the deck flexible without pushing explosive turns or lock patterns. Overall, the pass lowers swinginess and makes the game plan easier for a casual table to engage with.

added.behold the multiverse: [[Behold the Multiverse]] adds clean instant-speed card selection and draw without creating oppressive play patterns.
added.commit // memory: [[Commit // Memory]] is a versatile answer that interacts fairly while offering a late reset option.
added.fact or fiction: [[Fact or Fiction]] gives efficient card advantage in a way that feels interactive and table-friendly.
added.into the roil: [[Into the Roil]] provides simple, flexible bounce that can also replace itself.
added.mulldrifter: [[Mulldrifter]] is a casual-friendly value creature that draws cards without spiking power too hard.
added.pongify: [[Pongify]] trades a creature cleanly while leaving behind a manageable body.
added.rapid hybridization: [[Rapid Hybridization]] is efficient removal that answers threats without locking anyone out.
added.ravenform: [[Ravenform]] expands the deck's answers by cleanly exiling a creature or artifact.
added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] gives compact creature interaction that fits a lower-powered control shell.
added.resculpt: [[Resculpt]] offers flexible artifact or creature removal at a fair rate.
added.treasure cruise: [[Treasure Cruise]] adds a big refill effect that rewards normal game flow and resource trading.
added.wash out: [[Wash Out]] is a broad tempo play that resets boards without ending games on the spot.

removed.banishing knack: [[Banishing Knack]] is more combo-oriented than this casual pass wants.
removed.commandeer: [[Commandeer]] creates high-swing blowouts that can feel too punishing for casual games.
removed.harbinger of the seas: [[Harbinger of the Seas]] pressures mana bases in a way that can be frustrating at casual tables.
removed.high tide: [[High Tide]] pushes explosive mana turns that raise the deck's ceiling too much.
removed.leveler: [[Leveler]] mainly supports all-in combo lines rather than fair casual play.
removed.muddle the mixture: [[Muddle the Mixture]] is often used as a combo tutor here, so it was trimmed for a softer build.
removed.narset, parter of veils: [[Narset, Parter of Veils]] can shut down opposing card draw in a way many casual tables dislike.
removed.nexus mentality: [[Nexus Mentality]] was cut to make room for cleaner, more generally useful interaction.
removed.retraction helix: [[Retraction Helix]] overlaps with combo-style bounce lines that this pass is reducing.
removed.thought lash: [[Thought Lash]] is a narrow combo enabler that does not support the relaxed game plan.
removed.tidespout tyrant: [[Tidespout Tyrant]] can take over games through repetitive bounce loops and was too strong for the target power.
removed.valley floodcaller: [[Valley Floodcaller]] was removed to lower synergy pressure and keep the deck's play patterns simpler.

warnings: Model output had 107 cards after color filter; list trimmed to 100.
Budget guard removed newly added cards priced above about $35.
Budget guard kept estimated deck price around Moderate expectations.
results.summary: Transformed: More optimized. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~3.04.
results.why: This optimization pass trims excess basics and low-impact pieces to add cheaper interaction, cleaner tutoring, and stronger card-flow around [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]]. The changes make the deck more consistent at finding key spells while improving its ability to protect tempo. It also adds a stronger top-end draw package so the deck can convert mana and setup into a decisive advantage.

raw.summary: Transformed: More optimized. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~3.04.
raw.plan: This optimization pass trims excess basics and low-impact pieces to add cheaper interaction, cleaner tutoring, and stronger card-flow around [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]]. The changes make the deck more consistent at finding key spells while improving its ability to protect tempo. It also adds a stronger top-end draw package so the deck can convert mana and setup into a decisive advantage.
raw.why: This optimization pass trims excess basics and low-impact pieces to add cheaper interaction, cleaner tutoring, and stronger card-flow around [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]]. The changes make the deck more consistent at finding key spells while improving its ability to protect tempo. It also adds a stronger top-end draw package so the deck can convert mana and setup into a decisive advantage.

added.cyclonic rift: [[Cyclonic Rift]] gives the deck a flexible answer early and a powerful reset later.
added.enter the infinite: [[Enter the Infinite]] provides an extreme top-end payoff for a deck built to accumulate cards and mana.
added.fabricate: [[Fabricate]] improves consistency by directly finding an important artifact.
added.long-term plans: [[Long-Term Plans]] adds efficient library setup to access key spells when needed.
added.merchant scroll: [[Merchant Scroll]] tutors for important blue instants with low opportunity cost.
added.pongify: [[Pongify]] is cheap creature removal that improves the deck's interaction density.
added.rapid hybridization: [[Rapid Hybridization]] adds another efficient answer to problematic creatures.
added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] answers creatures cleanly while helping cover indestructible threats.
added.resculpt: [[Resculpt]] broadens removal by hitting creatures or artifacts at instant speed.
added.serum visions: [[Serum Visions]] smooths early draws and helps line up future turns.
added.solve the equation: [[Solve the Equation]] increases consistency by finding the exact instant or sorcery needed.
added.stroke of genius: [[Stroke of Genius]] turns excess mana into scalable card draw and a strong late-game payoff.
added.treasure cruise: [[Treasure Cruise]] offers very efficient refill in a deck that naturally stocks the graveyard.

removed.island: Cutting [[Island]] frees space after raising the deck's overall spell quality and utility.
removed.nexus mentality: [[Nexus Mentality]] was cut for a card with stronger consistency or interaction value.
removed.retraction helix: [[Retraction Helix]] was removed because it is narrower than the incoming upgrades.
removed.the unagi of kyoshi island: [[The Unagi of Kyoshi Island]] was cut for a more reliable and impactful effect.
removed.valley floodcaller: [[Valley Floodcaller]] was removed to prioritize cards that better support the deck's optimized spell plan.

warnings: Budget guard removed newly added cards priced above about $35.
Budget guard kept estimated deck price around Moderate expectations.

results.summary: No legality changes needed. This deck already passes current Modern legality and color identity checks.
results.why: No swaps were suggested because this deck already passes current Modern legality, ban-list, and copy-count checks.

cautions: General cleanup changed more than 24 cards; that feels aggressive for a cleanup pass.
warnings: General cleanup restored land count stability for this constructed deck.
results.summary: Transformed: General refinement. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.09.
results.why: This pass tightens the deck into a more focused Golgari sacrifice shell with cheaper interaction, steadier mana, and more consistent engine pieces. [[Gilded Goose]], [[Prosperous Innkeeper]], [[Witch's Oven]], and [[Trail of Crumbs]] improve early setup and resource flow, while [[Fatal Push]] and [[Deadly Dispute]] raise efficiency. The land updates also restore cleaner color access and more stable counts for a low-curve constructed build.

raw.summary: Transformed: General refinement. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.09.
raw.plan: This pass tightens the deck into a more focused Golgari sacrifice shell with cheaper interaction, steadier mana, and more consistent engine pieces. [[Gilded Goose]], [[Prosperous Innkeeper]], [[Witch's Oven]], and [[Trail of Crumbs]] improve early setup and resource flow, while [[Fatal Push]] and [[Deadly Dispute]] raise efficiency. The land updates also restore cleaner color access and more stable counts for a low-curve constructed build.
raw.why: This pass tightens the deck into a more focused Golgari sacrifice shell with cheaper interaction, steadier mana, and more consistent engine pieces. [[Gilded Goose]], [[Prosperous Innkeeper]], [[Witch's Oven]], and [[Trail of Crumbs]] improve early setup and resource flow, while [[Fatal Push]] and [[Deadly Dispute]] raise efficiency. The land updates also restore cleaner color access and more stable counts for a low-curve constructed build.

added.deadly dispute: [[Deadly Dispute]] turns expendable permanents into efficient card draw and treasure.
added.fatal push: [[Fatal Push]] gives the deck cheap, reliable creature interaction.
added.gilded goose: [[Gilded Goose]] adds early fixing, food support, and sacrifice utility.
added.prosperous innkeeper: [[Prosperous Innkeeper]] ramps while supplying food for the deck's engine.
added.witch's oven: [[Witch's Oven]] is a key repeatable sacrifice outlet that also makes food.
added.ygra, eater of all: [[Ygra, Eater of All]] is a strong payoff for the deck's food and sacrifice plan.
added.bake into a pie: [[Bake into a Pie]] adds removal that also contributes food.
added.boseiju, who endures: [[Boseiju, Who Endures]] offers flexible utility without costing a spell slot.
added.overgrown tomb: [[Overgrown Tomb]] strengthens the mana base with premium dual access.
added.trail of crumbs: [[Trail of Crumbs]] gives the deck a durable source of card selection and value.
added.village rites: [[Village Rites]] converts sacrifice fodder into very efficient card draw.
added.castle locthwain: [[Castle Locthwain]] provides extra card draw from the mana base.
added.cavern of souls: [[Cavern of Souls]] improves creature deployment through countermagic matchups.
added.nurturing peatland: [[Nurturing Peatland]] supports both colors and can cash in for a card later.
added.pendelhaven: [[Pendelhaven]] gives small utility creatures a useful combat boost.
added.takenuma, abandoned mire: [[Takenuma, Abandoned Mire]] adds low-cost recursion from a land slot.

removed.tender wildguide: [[Tender Wildguide]] was cut for more efficient sacrifice and food enablers.
removed.eusocial engineering: [[Eusocial Engineering]] was removed to lower clunky setup pieces.
removed.innkeeper's talent: [[Innkeeper's Talent]] did not support the refined core plan as efficiently.
removed.mutable explorer: [[Mutable Explorer]] was cut for cards with stronger synergy and rate.
removed.otterball antics: [[Otterball Antics]] was removed to streamline the deck away from lower-impact effects.
removed.stocking the pantry: [[Stocking the Pantry]] was replaced by more reliable card advantage engines.
removed.tolls of war: [[Tolls of War]] was cut to keep interaction and threats cheaper.
removed.tragic trajectory: [[Tragic Trajectory]] was removed for more efficient removal options.
removed.unfortunate accident: [[Unfortunate Accident]] was cut as the removal suite became more streamlined.
removed.dismal backwater: [[Dismal Backwater]] was removed for faster, cleaner color fixing.
removed.jungle hollow: [[Jungle Hollow]] was cut to improve untapped mana access.
removed.michelangelo, weirdness to 11: [[Michelangelo, Weirdness to 11]] did not fit the streamlined black-green core.
removed.mimeoplasm, revered one: [[Mimeoplasm, Revered One]] was off-plan for the focused sacrifice build.
removed.rat king, verminister: [[Rat King, Verminister]] was removed for tighter synergy pieces.
removed.zimone, all-questioning: [[Zimone, All-Questioning]] was cut to avoid stretching into off-plan colors and effects.
removed.avishkar raceway: [[Avishkar Raceway]] was removed to stabilize the mana base.
removed.captivating cave: [[Captivating Cave]] was cut for stronger color-producing lands.
removed.crossroads village: [[Crossroads Village]] was removed to improve mana consistency.
removed.godless shrine: [[Godless Shrine]] was cut because white mana is no longer needed.
removed.night market: [[Night Market]] was removed as part of the mana-base cleanup.
removed.plains: [[Plains]] was cut because the refined build does not need white sources.
removed.rogue's passage: [[Rogue's Passage]] was removed because it is too slow for this lower-curve plan.
removed.starting town: [[Starting Town]] was cut to make the land base more consistent.

warnings: General cleanup restored land count stability for this constructed deck.
Mana base pass removed non-mana additions that fell outside the pass scope.
Mana-base guard reverted unrelated non-mana changes from this pass.
Budget guard kept estimated deck price around Moderate expectations.
results.summary: Transformed: Mana base improvement. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.19.
results.why: This pass is focused strictly on improving the mana base with a cleaner, more reliable land swap. [[Zagoth Triome]] broadens color access and supports smoother fixing than [[Mirage Mesa]]. The change stays within the mana-base scope while fitting the deck's focused, moderate-budget plan.

results.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~1.9.
results.why: This pass tightens the curve by replacing slower, off-plan cards and clunky fixing with cheaper threats, cleaner interaction, and a more reliable black-green mana base. [[Pawpatch Recruit]], [[Valley Rotcaller]], and [[Vinereap Mentor]] add efficient early plays, while the land swaps make casting the deck's core cards more consistent. The result is a smoother Modern-focused build that applies pressure earlier and stumbles less on mana.

[[Pawpatch Recruit]], [[Valley Rotcaller]], and [[Vinereap Mentor]] add efficient early plays, while the land swaps make casting the deck's core cards more consistent. The result is a smoother Modern-focused build that applies pressure earlier and stumbles less on mana.

warnings: Interaction guard removed additions that were not clear answers or protection.
results.summary: Transformed: More interaction. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.05.
results.why: This pass increases cheap interaction so the deck can answer creatures and key noncreature plays earlier. [[Fatal Push]], [[Go for the Throat]], and [[Spell Pierce]] lower the opportunity cost of holding up answers while keeping the curve lean. The cuts trim lower-impact spells and a few mana sources to make room for more reliable disruption.

results.summary: Transformed: More casual. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.59.
results.why: This pass makes the deck more casual by trimming scattered colors, quirky build-arounds, and low-synergy pieces for a cleaner black-green core. [[Llanowar Wastes]], [[Woodland Cemetery]], [[Overgrown Tomb]], and [[Temple of Malady]] greatly improve consistency, while the creature and removal additions make the deck play more straightforwardly. The result stays focused but feels less gimmicky and easier to curve out with.

[[Llanowar Wastes]], [[Woodland Cemetery]], [[Overgrown Tomb]], and [[Temple of Malady]] greatly improve consistency, while the creature and removal additions make the deck play more straightforwardly. [[Llanowar Wastes]], [[Woodland Cemetery]], [[Overgrown Tomb]], and [[Temple of Malady]] greatly improve consistency, while the creature and removal additions make the deck play more straightforwardly. added.llanowar wastes: [[Llanowar Wastes]] gives reliable black-green fixing with minimal fuss.

added.glissa sunslayer: [[Glissa Sunslayer]] is a strong standalone threat that rewards normal combat.

added.sentinel of the nameless city: [[Sentinel of the Nameless City]] supplies steady board presence and card selection.
added.temple of malady: [[Temple of Malady]] adds fixing plus a small consistency boost from scry.

results.summary: Transformed: More optimized. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.03.
results.why: These swaps tighten the deck into a cleaner Golgari-focused shell with stronger mana, lower setup costs, and more consistent early plays. [[Llanowar Wastes]], [[Woodland Cemetery]], and [[Overgrown Tomb]] improve color access, while efficient cards like [[Deep-Cavern Bat]] and [[Sentinel of the Nameless City]] raise card quality at the same curve. The cuts remove off-color lands, slower speculative pieces, and lower-impact cards so the deck executes its plan more reliably.
